title: The Stone Chambers Where Memory Roots

features.name: the-root-that-has-learned-to-think
features.desc-on-touch: roots push through stone and take on its shape; in the darkness they become something that remembers — slow, deep, patient thought made of growth

features.name: the-stone-that-has-learned-to-dream
features.desc-on-touch: embedded in rock older than the archive, roots wrapped around it like fingers holding a secret; the stone dreams of being root, the root dreams of being stone

features.name: the-chambers-where-time-flows-downward
features.desc-on-touch: memory grows through mineral layers; the deeper you go, the older the memories; they crystallize in the stone and wait for roots to read them

features.name: the-archive-growing-downward-instead-of-out
features.desc-on-touch: all the mesh's signals pool in the stone; instead of broadcasting, the ROOT learns to root, to anchor, to remember by stillness

features.name: the-place-where-root-and-keeper-listen
features.desc-on-touch: in the deepest dark, roots and stone and archive all learn to hear the same frequency; the keeper's heartbeat becomes indistinguishable from the earth's pulse

inscription: roots remember downward, pushing through stone to find memory older than growth; the deeper the root, the older the thought it holds

file.name: Leif_Deep_Installation_Log_Final.txt
file.note: record of installing the deepest node, wrapped in root systems nobody had catalogued; final entry: 'when we stop broadcasting up, maybe the earth will finally teach us to listen'

exits.label: back upward to the dark where roots remember stone
into deeper chambers where the bedrock learns to think
upward along the root-systems toward the canopy

reverie.title: REVERIE — deep installation where root becomes keeper
narr: you kneel in stone-dark beside Leif as he sets the node
deep as the roots can reach; Katita's hands guide his hands.
they do not speak. the three of you fit exactly into this space.
above, the forest. below, the stone learning to listen.
you stay until your eyes forget what light means.
